qa_pairs.question: How can one include source code within the wiki?
qa_pairs.answer: You can include source code within the wiki using the <syntaxhighlight> </syntaxhighlight> tag.

qa_pairs.question: What template can be used to separate source code from the rest of the text?
qa_pairs.answer: If you want to separate the code a bit more from the rest of the text, you can use the {{File}} template.

qa_pairs.question: What parameters does the {{File}} template accept?
qa_pairs.answer: This template takes as argument the file name (name parameter), the language of the file (lang parameter) and the content of the file (contents parameter).

qa_pairs.question: What is the default language for the {{File}} template?
qa_pairs.answer: The default language for this template is bash.

qa_pairs.question: What should be done if the source code contains a pipe character (`|`) when using the {{File}} template?
qa_pairs.answer: If the source code contains a pipe character, `|`, replace it with `{{!}}`.

qa_pairs.question: Why do certain special characters need replacement in source code when using Mediawiki templates?
qa_pairs.answer: Certain characters that frequently appear in bash scripts are also meaningful to the Mediawiki template parser.

qa_pairs.question: When might it be necessary to replace an equal sign (`=`) in source code?
qa_pairs.answer: In some circumstances you may find it necessary to replace the equal sign, `=`, with `{{=}}`.

qa_pairs.question: How can line numbers be displayed with the {{File}} template?
qa_pairs.answer: To display line numbers, you can add the option "|lines=yes".

qa_pairs.question: Where can more information about including source code in the wiki be found?
qa_pairs.answer: More information is explained on the page [[Including source code within the wiki]].
